13261395:
Demirah

Demirah is one of the numerous massive trading cities that dot the Qadiran region of the Plains of Paresh, but is often overshadowed by the capital of Katheer. It is located at the south-western limits of the Plains of Paresh along the banks of the Pashman River estuary as it reaches the Inner Sea.
16841565:
Ranage's Circle

Ranage's Circle is a tight-knit circle of massive cypress trees in the heart of the Mwangi Jungle. It was planted 1,000 years ago by druids of the Jambala Jaeg tribe. The ancient magic of the circle protects against evil creatures and hostile wildlife, making it a safe resting place for those who know how to find it. The tree at the centre of the circle has a split trunk, which is wrapped tightly around a black stone. According to legend, the stone is the prison of Golokango, who is either a demon or a powerful jungle spirit. The central tree is barely alive, and according to the legend is a arboreal regent druid who sacrificed most of its life force to seal the prison. Some tales even claim that the guardian is a sibling of Dimari-Diji, the incredibly ancient arboreal regent of Osibu.

Among its guardians is Temteki, an earth elemental that resembles a tree.
30812795:
Primarch's Residence

The Primarch's Residence is surrounded by high stone walls and enormous gardens, possibly the most extensive in the city. It is in this majestic building that the Primarch should reside, though since Lord Gyr's disappearance, the building has fallen into disuse. It was built two millennia ago by one of the city's most tyrannical Primarchs, who forced the mages of the Arcanamirium to create illusory chambers within the grand structure. These chambers would remind the sovereign of places where he could relax and escape the city's clamor. It was this tyranny displayed by the Primarch that led the Arclords of Nex to attack the city during the Conjured Siege, where this Primarch perished. However, his successors lacked the restraint to refrain from using the building, for its allure is too great.

It was here that Gyr was last seen, and Acting Primarch Wynsal Starborn has refused to live there, locking the doors and placing the Starwatch on constant guard. Recently, a group of researchers disappeared after a joint expedition between Starwatch and the Pathfinder Society, and the divinations of Third Law Lord Utgar of Gyr have revealed that something is amiss with the illusions placed in the building, leading to theories that it has to do with their connections to the Crux of Nex.
41599945:
Bhula Prison

Bhula Prison, or Bhula Jail, is a prison located on an island off the southern coast of Vudra, in the most desolate reaches of its Falling Mountains region. The massive structure detains creatures and criminals who would profane the rest of Vudra's holy soil with their mere presence.

Bhula is not the first prison built for this purpose, but all of its predecessors were shuttered or destroyed after their prisoners were afflicted with horrifying psychic maladies.
50981395:
Port Freedom

Situated on the northern bank of the Korir River delta, Port Freedom is Vidrian's second port, behind the capital Anthusis.

The sandbanks of the delta mean the port cannot accommodate large, deep water ships, and are also a serious hazard to the river trade with Kalabuto. Barges travelling the river normally call upon the services of the Rivermen's Guild, native Mwangi pilots who are the only people skilled enough to reliably navigate the river.
53671005:
Mistress of Angels

The Mistress of Angels is a fine marble sculpture located in Magnimar's southern Ordellia district. It depicts Ordellia Whilwren, one of Magnimar's founders, scanning the horizon of the Varisian Gulf, seemingly looking out for danger. Whilwren was a cleric of Desna and one of Alcaydian Indros' colleagues at the founding of the city, and the statue is an occasional gathering place for Magnimarian Mystery Cultists.
68958795:
Karcau

4 locations:

  • Cormegi Manor
  • Dravod Knock
  • Vault of Tears
81367105:
House of Golden Veils

The House of Golden Veils is the largest bank in Kintargo and a temple to the god Abadar. It is located in the northeastern corner of the city's Temple Hill district. As of 4724 AR its high priest was Mhelrem Gesteliel.
82492565:
Kalios

Kalios is the capital of the nation of Midea located on the southern Kardaji Bay coastline of the country looking out over the Iblydan archipelago.
82607715:
Heidmarch Manor

5 locations:

  • House of Lords

  • Versade Villa
93544385:
Oroskirr

Oroskirr, also known as the Opal Island or the Ever-Watching Eye, is a strange opalescent dome that rises from the waters of the Nyvyrd in northern Iobaria. The dome glows and crackles with energy every night, and draws lightning to its surface during thunderstorms. Its surface has never been breached, but locals presume it is the source of the Nyvyrd's unusually warm waters.
97097615:
Szigo's Grove

Szigo's Grove is the home of Szigo, the cannibal warlock of the Grungir Forest, who eats his victims in order to gain power. Many adventurers have attempted to destroy this deranged madman, but Szigo has a sizeable following of violent trolls and other monstrous creatures that Szigo has convinced that he is a god.

Szigo's minions serve him as both hunter-gatherers and guards for the horde of magical treasure he has amassed over the years. The most famous item in this horde is probably the magical sword of Laughing Erik, the hero who supposedly came closest to vanquishing the terrible cannibal.
98846725:
Alendruan Harbor

4 locations:
  • Baydac Falls
  • Eel's Skull
  • Obelisk of Wonders
  • Queen's Processional
100023135:
Swamp Village

Situated in the Narlmarch Woods, the Swamp Village is a generous name for what amounts to nothing more than two dilapidated stone buildings on the eastern bank of the Skunk River. Nonetheless, these long-abandoned structures have recently been claimed by a boggard named Garuum and his pet slurk Ubagub.
101668965:
Fort Hazard

Fort Hazard stands on the bluffs overlooking Port Peril, capital of the Shackles. Its strategic position allows it to control both the town and the harbour. It was the stronghold of the late former Hurricane King Kerdak Bonefist. The tallest of its seven defensive towers is also the location of the lighthouse known as Besmara's Beacon.
105886625:
Kizobran

As of 4713 AR, Kizobran was a small town on the Gullik River in the Irriseni province of Hoarwood. The town caters to merchant traffic on the river. Since a few years prior to 4713 AR, the ruler of the town was Baroness Veviane, who had quickly proven to be a harsh mistress for Kizobran by squeezing outrageous taxes from its peasants. She was also known to take a fancy to a man or woman, and when her carnal appetite was sated her victim was usually found frozen in a snow bank or floating in the Gullik River.

Baroness Veviane was in fact the offspring of a boreal green hag and a charmed Jadwiga man, substituted for Hedvika's real child by a midwife who was also in thrall to the hag. She murdered these "parents", Baroness Hedvika and Baron Gorney, by setting fire to their home, then succeeded them to the rulership of Kizobran.

The true daughter of Baroness Hedvika and Baron Gorney is Kataryne Vilimanov, an officer in the local peasant militia. Kataryne had recently discovered her true identity as of 4713 AR, and was secretly planning to pursue her revenge and restore her proper position in the aristocracy.

Sigvor Yovenovich was an assistant priest at the local temple of Pharasma. She was a member of the underground anti-government movement Heralds of Summer's Return and approached Kataryne in 4712 AR. Kataryne refused to join the Heralds, and Sigvor lives in fear that Kataryne might expose her.

As of 4719 AR, Kizobran was within the borders of the Realm of the Mammoth Lords.
106604215:
Glacier's Rest

Glacier's Rest is a small town in the foothills of the Stormspear Mountains of northwestern Hagreach in the Lands of the Linnorm Kings. It is five miles south of a long-abandoned dwarven fortress, which as of 4724 AR had been claimed by General Ordulf Bladecaller and named Crownhold.
113529505:
Eto

3 locations:
  • Floater's Pond
  • Hall of Seven Scepters
  • Sandfire Catacombs
142339235:
Emperor's Footprint

The Emperor's Footprint is a massive sinkhole in the southern Chang Liao Jungle of the Tian Xia nation of Po Li. It is a sight of religious pilgrimage, but it rarely visited because of the many dangerous creatures and hostile Sunsu Godae tribespeople who live there. Those who survive the journey to the Emperor's Footprint find that prayer and meditation connects one directly to one's patron deity in Heaven. A rare purple flower that holds resurrection or geas powers, and only blooms once every 144 years, is also said to grow here.
145646145:
Litran

Litran is a Galtan city best known for being the headquarters of the mysterious executioners known as the Gray Gardeners, and because of their intimidating presence, it was one of the most stable cities in the nation.
159862505:
Rapier Bay

Rapier Bay is a rowdy small town on Motaku Isle in the Shackles. It is led by the pirate lord Maxevale Janis, captain of the galleon Motaku Maiden. Maxevale always supports Lady Tessa in the Pirate Council, earning the nickname "the Puppy", though no man dares to say it to his face.

Rapier Bay is not a busy harbor like Quent, but is used by pirates who do not want to draw much attention to themselves while sailing near the island. Rapier Bay is famous for one of its smithies, the Steel-Clad Giant, that makes some of the finest blades west of the Arch of Aroden.
181010135:
Robu

Robu is a small town on the second largest island of the Ushinawa Isles in the Shackles.

The residents of Robu have done their best to eradicate the obscene reminders of its earlier Ghol-Gan occupants. A native evil, the penanggalan named Tiktakara, has laired in the woodlands outside of Robu and entertains herself by tormenting the town's residents and converting its women into subservient manananggals. The Lord of Robu, Kensai Hikarogawa, offers a bounty to any adventurers who can rid the town of this menace.
201060395:
Cathedral of Claws

The Cathedral of Claws is an ancient site in Thuvia that predates the Age of Legend. It is located at the foot of the mountain Elzehan in the Barrier Wall range east of the Thuvian city of Pashow,
206766935:
Pale Mountain

The Pale Mountain is located in northwest Katapesh near the junction of the Brazen Peaks and the Barrier Wall. It is avoided by travelers as numerous kholo tribes are active in the area. The Carrion King is the ruler of these tribal worshipers of Rovagug.
217902675:
Grand Lodge

221476465:
Nystra

Nystra is a ghost-town in the far-eastern reaches of the River Kingdoms. Once a thriving community centered around the cultivation and production of the fine silk produced by the insectoid silk gargoyles, or silkgoyles, its inhabitants were totally wiped out in a revenge plot by the Taldan druid Tharl Grimull in 4664 AR.
222560415:
Balmore's Canal

243946955:
Chorak's Tomb

A small island in Lake Skotha on Varisia's Storval Plateau, Chorak's Tomb is sacred to the region's giants. Many believed that it was the last outpost of a once-great giant civilization dating back thousands of years. However, all trespassers are driven away by bombardments of boulders and rune-covered ballista bolts fired by stone giant guardians of the site.

The tomb is the resting place of Chorak, a gigas. In 4724 AR, the tomb was struck by a warshard that awakened Chorak and began disabling the tomb's wards, despite the efforts of its stone giant guardians.

Arazlant Mox

4 locations:

265586835:
Sosulka

Sosulka is a small town on the Marbleflow River in Irriseni province of Wintercrux. It is located between the towns of Veshtak in the south and Iarna in the north.
266256445:
Blacksulfur Pond

Blacksulfer Pond is a still, black pond southwest of Nisroch. No rivers or stream feed into it, no fish or other aquatic life inhabit it, and no plants grow near it.
283881145:
Hospice of Serenity

The Hospice of Serenity is an abbey nestled among the slums of the Bilges, dedicated to a saint of healing. The nuns who run the hospice are easily recognizable by their shaved heads and white robes, and they offer healing services to the poorest of the poor. Sister Maeri is the leader here and secretly a member of the Red Mantis, offering healing services in exchange for information, new recruits, or to sell the location of fugitives to parties that can pay. Since there aren't many temples in the city, most adventurers go to her, which keeps her informed of potentially dangerous groups in the city.
298891665:
Elara's Halfway House

Elara's Halfway House was an orphanage in Darkmoon Vale located on a hill at the edge of Darkmoon Wood eight miles from Falcon's Hollow. It burned down under suspicious circumstances around 4707 AR with everyone within already dead, leaving it a fire-blackened ruin.
309290335:
Sunstone Cliffs

The Sunstone Cliffs lie in the very north-eastern corner of the Thanelands in the Lands of the Linnorm Kings in the foothills of the Stormspear Mountains range. The cliffs are famous for the gemstones found there called Thrain sunstones, unique gems that glow with a warm yellow light and whose flaws allow knowledgeable users to determine which way is north. Named for the prospector who discovered them, Thrain sunstones vary from smaller, inferior gems with the brightness of a candle to those that glow like a lantern and any jewellery made from sunstones will fetch huge prices across the Inner Sea region.

Unfortunately, the Sunstone Cliffs are a very dangerous region since they serve as the hunting ground for large groups of trolls and even rock trolls making it particularly dangerous at night. Those who come here to prospect for Thrain sunstones have to harvest them with great stealth and in very quick runs lest they end up as troll food.
312928835:
Garden of Lead

The Garden of Lead, also known as the Pleasure Palace of the Dead, is a horrifically haunted place even by the standards of Virlych. It hosts endless "parties" among pools of molten metal where tortured ghosts of costumed revelers and undead servants provide grotesque entertainment for their dread mistress, the Skeleton Countess Dissayn.
313282315:
Justnoque

Justnoque is a sunken city underneath the Steaming Sea, off the coast of Varisia and the Lands of the Linnorm Kings. In the times of ancient Thassilon, Justnoque was a center of learning and metallurgy in the kingdom of Cyrusian, but it now lies ruined under the waves; only about a dozen slick black stones jutting from the water mark its presence. The sunken city is feared by the Linnorm Kings, but adventurers sometimes set sail from the Linnorm King city of Jol to explore Justnoque; the few that return tell tales of aquatic giants inhabiting the ruins.
313914155:
Khulo

The seaside town of Khulo, the only settlement on Valkus Isle, was once a favorite pleasure resort of the nobility of Nex. Failed experiments carried out in the island's interior by the archmage Nex saw the island overrun by dangerous planar forces and monsters, with unfortunate results for the town's population. The chaos meant that the settlement was run by crime lords and spirit nagas vying for power.

Today, the town (like the rest of the island) is cut off from civilization by the protective Stalwart Wall, so it is unknown what creatures (if any) currently dwell there.
313914585:
Baphon's Fall

Baphon's Fall is a waterfall approximately 1,800 feet high, colloquially named after the belief that Aroden defeated Tar-Baphon in the area. The waterfall's water originates from the Whispering River, which carries the void energy produced by the storms surrounding The Three Furies. As a result, the water is harmful to living beings, and the vegetation near the falls is blackened and lifeless.
316439245:
Graymoor

Graymoor is a relatively peaceful town in Sovereign's Reach, a region of Numeria known more for its history of war and technological horrors. Graymoor is one of the few places on Golarion where androids are welcomed and can live alongside the locals peaceably.
348697015:
Sze

The town of Sze is one of the settlements scattered throughout the desolate, monster-haunted land of Shenmen. It has been ruled for centuries by a cruel ghostly governor named Chou Mingxia, whose pointless pursuit of lumber is driven by her refusal to acknowledge the fall of Lung Wa.
349213655:
Azure Star

Azure Star is a popular restaurant deep in the Lower City district of Katapesh. From the outside it is a large gray tent covered in sky-blue stars. Inside the tent, private rooms are formed using silk partitions, and all rooms surround a large open common room with overlapping carpets covering the floor. Patrons lounge around small tables, on soft comfortable pillows. Musicians and singers entertain the guests who come to taste the exotic cuisine served there such as honeyed loci, curdled camel milk, pesh-sprinkled dhabba liver, pickled scorpion, grilled adder, thrice-poisoned oysters, and others. A bound air elemental creates a soft breeze to comfort Azure Star's guests.
349676205:
Scrawny Crossing

The abandoned hamlet of Scrawny Crossing is in the western River Kingdoms. It served as a ferry crossing and caravan stop until 4706 AR, when its inhabitants disappeared in mysterious circumstances.

Unbeknownst to the folk of the region, Scrawny Crossing now serves as a base for a band of aquatic slavers—scrag/ulat-kini cross-breeds. These monsters capture humanoids for use in strange experiments. Their leaders are a naga ("Lord Slissk"), a scrag sorcerer ("Thult-Lorth") and a sea hag ("Mother Slug"), although they in turn take their orders from a telepathic creature that might be an alghollthu master.

Stranger still, next to the ruins of Scrawny Crossing's old smith sits an oddity known as the Boiling Well, a well whose water always came out at body temperature. Unbeknownst to anyone living there, this was because the well harboured a secret soulgate that linked to the Infernal Duke Eaqueo's domain of the Finishing Knot on Hell's first layer, Avernus.
354990415:
An-Alak

An-Alak is a small community on Osirion's Scorpion Coast that includes the Temple of An-Alak, dedicated to the god Irori.
375147575:
Numijaan

Numijaan is the mostly ruined capital of Wanshou in Tian Xia. A pale shadow of its former self, Numijaan once boasted more than 170,000 residents before a series of disastrous storms and the emergence of the elder kraken Zhanagorr laid waste to it.
386038715:
Sember Cove

The village of Sember Cove is nestled just inside the Ravounel border at the point it meets the Hellcoast in Cheliax.
386054055:
Naamat

Naamat is a luxurious tent city located in the Northern Zho Mountains region of Qadira on the banks of the River Ladan. The Northern Zho Mountains region is the poorest region in Qadira and faces the neighbouring land of Taldor just across the Jalrune River. As such, it is the least hungry for war with their neighbors.
390112415:
Celwynvian

The ancient elven capital city of Celwynvian (pronounced sehl-WIN-vee-enn) sits amid the Mierani Forest in northern Varisia. Abandoned by the elves when they departed Golarion prior to the Earthfall, the city became overridden with drow, and was the site of one of the most vicious conflicts between the two elven races upon their return from Sovyrian. Taking considerable losses from the drow's evil tactics, the elves once again departed the city, and it remains empty and forbidden to this day.

The elves of the Mierani Forest strictly prohibit outsiders from entering the city, under the guise of wanting to preserve it as a refuge from the rest of the world, but those more familiar with the region sense that the elves themselves avoid the city and are still involved in an ongoing war to reclaim it.
390550965:
Nagisa

Nagisa is a ruined city on the eastern part of the Bdonge River in the Mwangi Expanse. It is also known as the White City, as its buildings—a number of terraced pyramids, surrounded by smaller structures—are built entirely from white stone.
397568075:
Hesheq

Hesheq is the capital of the nation of Khattib located on the coast of the Betul Sea.
400117825:
Zelen

Zelen is a peasant settlement on the Rimeflow River in the westernmost Irriseni province known as the Verge. It handles merchant barges heading eastward to Algidheart or Whitethrone, or westward to Iceflow. Most river captains choose Zelen to sleep in a warm bed for at least one night. Baroness Pribuska is the ruler of Zelen. She was a paramour of Duke Arvanoff, and nourishes great hatred for Baroness Slavena of Skrata who has replaced her in the duke's affections in 4710 AR. The waters of the Rimeflow near Zelen are rich with fish and the town sends a large portion of its catch as far as south as Redtooth. The Vasterborg Run is a caravan of dogsleds led by Raisa Gevenovna and Zavid Menavich that makes the challenging monthly trip to Redtooth.

401862065:
Graydirge

Graydirge is a small, remote, and somber ossuary-city in the foothills of the Shattered Range in north-central Geb. The normal custom of Geb is to re-animate its citizens as undead once they die. Graydirge is literally built out of the bones of those Gebbites who were unwilling or unable to be reanimated. Its inhabitants, no doubt, have their own reasons for living in a city-sized ossuary. Its one notable feature is the Empty Threshold, a temple of Zon-Kuthon. It is home to only one Blood Lord, Berline Haldoli.

Surrounded by a 30-foot wall of fused stone and bone, this city has three main gates to the east, south, and west.
402335665:
Kho

The ruins of Kho were once the first flying city of the Shory Empire. Located in the Kho-Rarne Pass of the Barrier Wall Mountains between the Mwangi Expanse and Osirion, the former Shory capital fell to earth millennia ago.

416766065:
Vladmirr

Vladmirr is an isolated Iobarian town on the shores of the Castrovin Sea. The southernmost major settlement in Iobaria, Vladmirr is surrounded by the delta of the Pharrus River, close to the Icerime Peaks. Though the town is rich in natural resources with a bounty of arable lands and precious minerals, its lack of a protected port and the Castrovin's high waves often leaves the town overlooked in favour of Kridorn or Mirnbay.
421752005:
Cerulean Docks

The Cerulean Docks are small docks where ships disembark in Kerse, though their size is normal given that they are decorated with marble pillars and precious stones throughout. Rhivash is the dock chief, and it is his efforts that keep the hundreds of ships moving through the area each day in perfect motion.

465203675:
Truther & Trathis Mining and Excavation Company

A nondescript building in the city of Katapesh serves as the office of Truther & Trathis Mining and Excavation Company, a mining and excavation enterprise maintained by two dwarves, Gravlet Truther and Kip Trathis. The pair are notoriously spendthrift with their money and refuse to pay anyone else to do work that they can't do themselves.

The company usually has a variety of operations in process at any given time, and are working on reopening the Zolurket Mines despite the rumors of undead there, and excavating the region around the Crouching Jackal. They claim to have come across an ancient scroll depicting other structures around the statue, and think that their excavation efforts will reveal other lost structures buried beneath the sand.

The truth behind the Zolurket Mines operation is that it is, in fact, haunted by the two dwarves' dead business partner, Tibbler Rocksledge, abandoned decades ago to a mummy attack when the trio was first exploring the mines. The ghost of Tibbler haunts the mines in anticipation of the return of Truther and Trathis, who have returned several times searching for rich platinum deposits buried there.

The current mining operation is their most ambitious to date: they've employed diggers to tunnel through the rock around their ghostly foe, but the work goes slowly and the two dwarves are growing increasingly frustrated. They are ready to hire a group of specialize ghost hunters to take out Tibbler permanently, despite the extra costs and hassle involved in employing adventurers. What they haven't realized is that the only way to permanently destroy the ghost is to satisfy its need to bring the pair to justice.
467592195:
Fifth Wind

The Fifth Wind is a stone weather vane that rises from the Dockway district in Magnimar. The sides feature carvings of air elementals and cloud dragons. All of Outcast's Cove can determine the speed and direction by looking up at the windsocks. Those who spend time observing this monument are rumored to avoid seasickness.
473439065:
Reliant Book Company

Reliant Book Company was the name of a bookstore in Breachill that, as of 4719 AR, sold antique magic tomes and adventurers' journals from decades past. The owner was Voz Lirayne, and her apprentice, Calmont Trenault, performed more ordinary tasks. The bookstore was quite small, and its specialized collection meant it received few customers.
489304705:
Ngōn Hoa

Ngōn Hoa (pronounced ng-ON hwah) is the ornate, soaring capital of Xa Hoi and one of Tian Xia's largest cities.
498724685:
Crypt of Angus Amberleg

The Crypt of Angus Amberleg is believed to be the resting place of not only the notorious and successful pirate, who ravaged the Inner Sea and plundered 100 ships, but also his treasure. It is located somewhere in the interior of Mediogalti Island and is arguably the most famous of the region's numerous pirate crypts.

According to legend, the crypt was built by slaves who became a permanent part of the construction by being buried alive once the work was finished. It is believed that their ghosts now guard the treasure along with the undead pirate himself.

The treasure is believed to include ancient artefacts and large jewels, some the size of an ogre's fist.
505290205:
Kridorn

Kridorn is the second-largest city in Iobaria, passed only by its regional rival of Mirnbay. Located on sloping cliffs that rise above the Castrovin Sea, Kridorn's towers and lighthouse make it an easily visible landmark for sailors.

Founded in 905 AR, Kridorn was originally a small fishing town, but its growth was stymied by pirate fleets. King Iobar I attempted to drive off the pirates, but ended up perishing at the Battle of Kridorn in 937 AR. Hrugil, Iobar's son, repelled the invading corsairs, and spent the remainder of his reign improving Iobaria's fleets, resulting in Kridorn's growth into a booming port city by 975 AR. Kridorn was able to survive New Iobaria's collapse in 4659 AR, and became the destination of many Galtan nobles fleeing their nation's collapse into revolution.

Kridorn is considered one of Iobaria's safest cities, despite unreliable services and rampant corruption caused by its leader's thirst for conquest.
512035005:
Korradath

The Korradath is the former royal castle and seat of Imperial Cheliax in the Rego Corna section of Westcrown. A massive fortified castle, it remains an impressive sight even though it has not seen any major upkeep since before the death of King Gaspodar in Neth of 4622 AR. It is still used by Wiscrani nobles for offices, temporary lodgings, or neutral meeting places for visitors to Westcrown that need to be impressed and protected from the rabble of the streets.

Aaramor

The city-fortress of Aaramor is located on the southern banks of the West Sellen River in northwestern Numeria, near the edge of Sarkoris.

541629595:
City of Bone and Juniper

The City of Bone and Juniper is a taiga giant settlement in the mountains of Zi Ha in Tian Xia.

The giants believe they are descendants of an underworld dragon and an ancient samsaran noble who sought to escape the cycle of rebirth to remain with her beloved. They keep the remains of their samsaran progenitor in a golden mausoleum on a hidden plateau marked by three towers made of wood and bone high in the mountains that happens to be located at the intersection of two ley lines, which provide the plateau special magical properties. Anyone who dares approach the mausoleum will be slain.

The taiga giants spend most of the time dressed in red and black, but occasionally honor the samsaran noble by dressing in mottled blue and white. During the latter times they travel peacefully to the lowlands to trade with the human and samsaran villagers, all the while maintaining a strict code of silence.

  • Voinum Estate
555608585:
Screaming Obelisk

A landmark of the Inner City district of Katapesh, The Screaming Obelisk is visible for miles around. The Screaming Obelisk is a massive spire nearly 400 feet tall and made of solid red carnelian.

The structure is covered in symbols; the one nearest the bottom seems to be times and date, while the higher, more mysterious symbols are unfanthomable. This does not stop people trying, and every year the Obelisk attracts scholars, linguists, engineers, and wizards from around the Inner Sea region who try to unravel its mysteries. The Screaming Obelisk's name comes from the holes cut into the top of it which, when the winds pick up, radiate an eerie series of whistling sounds.

As well as an impressive tourist attraction, the Obelisk also serves as a giant sundial. Surrounding the Screaming Obelisk are three belltowers that use the shadow cast by the Obelisk to measure the passage of time, sounding a bell at every hour of the day.
555932795:
Citadel Altaerein

Citadel Altaerein is the abandoned former headquarters of the Hellknight Order of the Nail, located in eastern Isger, high in the northwestern Five Kings Mountains, close to the border with Druma. It overlooks the town of Breachill, whose residents call it Hellknight Hill and largely ignore it.
561183275:
Shotep-Kara

Shotep-Kara on the Scorpion Coast of Osirion was a military and commercial hub connecting Tumen to the Obari Ocean. The port city lost its prominent role after the death of the Four Pharaohs and the ruin of Tumen. A number of earthquakes and tsunamis finally drew the city into the sea in 1687 AR.

Shotep-Kara's ancient monuments and temples are now part of the underwater ruins, and the place remains imbued with powerful magic. At the height of Four Pharaohs' reign, conjuration magic connected the port to the far corners of the empire and beyond. The magic is believed to persist to this day, though in wildly unstable form. Oceanic horrors and unquiet spirits infest the waters, but Shotep-Kara might still offer magical passage to faraway places, or even to other planes.
565766925:
Sunset Imports

3 locations:
  • Silver Suppers
  • Vashnarstill Shipyard
  • War Cage
567477025:
Ophidian Estate

The Ophidian Estate is located outside the walls of Kerse and serves as the Aspis Consortium's base of operations in the city. They wish to improve their relations with the Prophecies of Kalistrade, perhaps even by placing one of their members in the order, or vice versa. Currently, operations are being led by Brinesie Iliesco, who is seeking guards to protect the trade routes connecting to Andoran.
610424525:
Sallix Salt Works

2 locations:
  • Odde & Daughter Herbs
  • Salt Market
613882635:
Frostbreach

Frostbreach is a small but self-sufficient town in the northern Lands of the Linnorm Kings, on the Path of Aganhei. Once a more prosperous settlement, its population has since shrunk to about a third of its peak. It was the site of the Jarlsblood Witch Trial in centuries past, in which the prospective Linnorm King Kolvi killed the reigning Alvard the Wolf in claimed self-defense, but was accused of being an Irriseni witch and executed.
629999675:
Maroux's Isle

4 locations:
  • Battlefield
  • Cavern of Many Mouths
  • Troglodyte Caves
  • Wreck of the Impdrake
644883105:
Boali

The ruined city of Boali stands on the northern shore of the Bay of Senghor on Garund's western coast, north of the city-state of Senghor and south of the pirate haven of Bloodcove.
662325295:
Fancy Reefclaw

The Fancy Reefclaw is a gaudily painted, but successful, brewery located in Magnimar's Lowcleft district. The front is painted red and yellow, with an image of a well-dressed reefclaw. Owned by House Deverin, it is operated day-to-day by Golliver Haskinswurth. The brewery is best known for its raspberry ale and lambics.
666351235:
Rust Gate

The Rust Gate straddles the border between Kintargo's districts of Old Kintargo and Redroof and is the city's most heavily trafficked gate. Most of the traffic crossing through it comes from the Ravounel Road, the busiest roadway in the city, which leads to Kintargo from the south and over the Argo Bridge.
687763765:
Silver Mount

The jagged metallic peak of the Silver Mount is the above-ground remnant of the largest of the fallen starships that crashed in Numeria during the legendary Rain of Stars thousands of years ago. The edifice is honeycombed with chambers and was once heavily guarded by servants of the since-defunct Technic League.

This scarred monolith looms over the capital city of Starfall and the strange knowledge and technology found therein is the source of much of the Black Sovereign's (and Technic League's) power. Even though Silver Mount is closely guarded by the soldiers of the Black Sovereign, certain brave scholars and adventurers continue to try and enter the structure in the hopes of discovering untold wealth or technology from beyond this world.
689686165:
Halvon

Halvon is a town in northern Galt, just north of the Boarwood. On the Night of the Gray Death, nearly 2,000 of its residents were killed by a gray death released from the town's final blade, laying waste to Halvon. As of 4725 AR, only 263 residents remain, and the town continues to decline.
691795875:
Wachail Estate

The Wachail Estate serves as the residence of the decaying noble family led by Lord Navvem. It was raided in 4720 AR after he refused to free his slaves when the practice was outlawed. The stone house resembles a fortress, and since then the family has sought alliances with all manner of unsavory individuals in an attempt to recapture their former glory.
703605895:
Shadow Clock

  • Golemworks

  • Harpy

715369955:
Old Detmer

Old Detmer is the nickname given by the locals to the underwater ruins of the city of an ancient civilization. Standing stones and antimagic wards scattered around the area make it difficult to explore fully.
716366765:
Dragonsthrall

Dragonsthrall is the only settlement on the island of Dahak's Horn. The island of Dahak's Horn was chosen by the great dragon-priest Aashaq when she first came to the isles of the Shackles, for her loyal humanoid worshipers to inhabit.
722111425:
Arvensoar

3 locations:
  • Defiant's Garden
  • Gilded Cage
  • Temple of Iomedae
728878545:
Watercleft

The Watercleft is a cliff on the border between the Petal District and Eastgate, where a spring is moved by aqueducts, creating a beautiful view. There is much social activity in the area as people come to collect water from the spring. The distribution of water through private tunnels is seen as a disgrace by the residents of Green Ridge, which was once a separate district from Eastgate with its own council. After being absorbed, the people of Green Ridge lamented the loss of the values ​​that made the Watercleft so appealing.
728914405:
Hillview Station

Hillview Station is the Lotus Guard's base of operations and the Petal District courthouse, hosting the city's highest-profile trials. Inside, the galleries offer spacious viewing areas and elevated balconies for observing the proceedings, and the holding cells resemble luxury suites compared to other jails in the city.
738520025:
An

An is the first of Sothis' trio of southern sister cities, along with Tephu and Wati. It was founded in -107 AR by Pharaoh Hirkoshek I as a worker city housing the laborers and artisans working to construct his pyramid.

The City of Triangles is so-called because of the similar shapes of distant Mount Na-Ken and the surrounding peaks, as well as the pyramids visible on the southern horizon. Aside from the pyramid of Hirkoshek I, the region also holds the pyramids of Hirkoshek II, Zahur I, and Kamaria the Brazen. Kamaria's pyramid, thought plundered long ago, continues to attract followers of the cult of Rovagug, and some whisper that there may be unopened levels beneath those already explored.
739897675:
Moonpool Causeways

The Moonpool Causeways are a series of magical pools ranging in width from a few feet to half a mile located in the northeastern Fierani Forest of Kyonin. The pools have properties that are similar to common uses of chonomancy and the dragon Ekhazarean has been studying it for decades.

The pristine pools were besieged by omoxs sent by Treerazer to corrupt and destroy the fey that were allied with Kyonin but the magical defenses proved too much and the fey were able to withstand the assault.
742741885:
Embassy Building

1 locations:
  • Rose and Rake Theater
744296345:
Yensa

The border town of Yensa lies on the Iceflow River, separating the Irrisen province of Bleakmarch from the Linnorm Kingdom of Hagreach. Its ruler, Countess Marcelyna, does her best to encourage trade between the two nations, even going so far as to bar entrance to the town to mercenary companies and other ruffians intent on conducting cross-border raids.

Countess carefully cultivates a friendly image is order to foster commerce but the Yensa's inhabitants know their mistress' cruel nature. None dare to reveal the truth to visitors who hear nothing but praise for the countess from her secretly brutalized subjects. The blacksmith Vanak Usztony lost his wife from Marcelyna's cruelty and he still does not speak a word of complain in order to protect his four children.
757313005:
Ryvanthnyl

Ryvanthnyl is a memorial located in the eastern part of Kyonin. The site is dedicated to the lives and traditions of people and cultures that perished and are remembered by the elves who preserve their memory by leaving behind works of art and objects of significance.

Ryvanthnyl is protected by gravekeepers and preserved through magic that safeguards the objects within, though the origin, era, and historical significance of many of these objects remain unknown.
800450625:
Treaclerun

The estate of Treaclerun is home to the ruler of Kazuhn Prefecture, the Grand Duchess Mellea Denzarni, and is named for the decadent parties her ancestors used to throw there: parties where fountains of honey flowed like water. While appearing as little more than a hedonistic hunting lodge to escape from the strife of Taldan politics, in reality, Treaclerun has a bloody history. Serving as the breadbasket of Taldor's capital, Oppara, rulership of Kazuhn has generally fallen to only the most ruthless of Taldor's power players. The estate serves as a discrete location for shady deeds and sinister schemes. Almost every noble estate in Taldor boasts a handful of ghost stories associated with it, Treaclerun is a rare exception: no lingering spectres haunt its ground. Rumour has it this is due to some strange but deliberate quirk of the estate's design.
806276295:
Painter's Pot

The Painter's Pot is a region of the Darkmoon Plain where high-quality clay can be obtained, and painters and sculptors travel to the area regularly. Those who travel there should be accompanied by a guide from either the Fangwatch or the Greenfire Circle, as the area is the territory of a magmin tribe that can be dangerous.
813299085:
Cairn of Attai Horse-Speaker

The Cairn of Attai Horse-Speaker is the tomb of an ancient Nidalese horselord, now overrun by sinister fey.
831607645:
Ollo

The town of Ollo is one of the largest settlements in the Shackles. It is ruled by the cruel pirate Avimar Sorrinash, a member of the Pirate Council.

Ollo is on Shark Island and is plagued by raids from the sedacthys who live in the sea near the island. These raids are carried out with Sorrinash's permission, and the people of Ollo live in daily fear of a sedacthy attack.
832020705:
Exemplary Execrables

834918915:
Trollclaw Cleft

Trollclaw Cleft is a cavern where a tribe of jinkins, under the orders of the Mother of Flies, cared for an enormous beetle that had been raised by the hag since it was young and was, to say the least, powerful.
840022615:
Battle of Grasyhot

The site of the Battle of Grasyhot can be found northeast of the Numerian capital of Starfall, just south of the Egelsee River near the border with Mendev. In it, the forces of the Kellid Queen Boliga Bharsolm fought with the 30 tribes of the hill giant chief Urgo Axbiter. The battle began in 1288 AR and continued on and off for three years, with both sides experiencing advances and retreats. When Axbiter was killed, his followers erected a huge earthen mound over his grave near the battlefield, so that he might continue to inspire them in death. The climactic confrontation of the battle ended in victory for the Kellid tribes. Queen Bharsolm met her death during it, and was interred in a similar earthen tomb at the battle site, so that she might eternally delight in the hill giants' defeat. Both burial mounds still stand today, a lasting tribute to heroism and determination.
846747975:
Shrine of Aroden

The Shrine of Aroden is one of several temples that were abandoned after the god's death, and since Westcrown was one of the centers of his faith, there are quite a few in this state. Although the Church of Iomedae theoretically owns the buildings, they lack the funds and personnel to pay for the necessary renovations. Various rebel groups have used the temples as safe houses in the past.
848218355:
Sumadhadra

Sumadhadra is a massive port city located on the easternmost tip of Vudra's Divine Garden region, where it meets the Embaral Ocean, and is the subcontinent's easternmost city. Sumadhadra is a prominent trade centre, particularly with the continent of Tian Xia, and is where most visitors from that continent first enter Vudra.
848664935:
Bone Garden

The Bone Garden is an unusual kholo cemetery located in the foothills of the Brazen Peaks along Osirion's southern border with Katapesh. Firstly, it is unusual, because most kholo do not bury their dead, preferring to leave them to be picked apart by hyenas and vultures. Secondly, the Bone Garden is unusual because those who are interred here do not stay in the ground for long. This is because the graveyard is tended by kholo priestesses of Urgathoa, who are happy to accept the bodies of any fallen kholo brought here by its tribe-mates and convert them to skeletons and zombies. The devotees of the Pallid Princess have amassed a sizable army of the undead, which they trade with the Pharaoh of Fangs to augment his troops.
851387905:
Xin-Cyrusian

Xin-Cyrusian was the capital of Cyrusian, the Thassilonian realm of pride, and that empire's largest city. It was said to have been protected by legions of bound angels, dragons, and enslaved giants. It was located in the western Kodar Mountains.
860375025:
Spiralcross Cemetery


The Spiralcross Cemetery is the largest and most prestigious cemetery in all of Absalom, where Primarch]s, nobles, and high priests are buried. Located next to the Ahnkamen Estate, this cemetery is a labyrinth of vaults and shrines belonging to families with over four millennia of history. A large church of Pharasma stands among the tombs, and its presence, along with the proximity to Mother Jackal, means that few undead awaken in the area.

There are several ghost stories about the place, but only one seems true: white orbs are seen moving through the cemetery at night, and it is said that they may be codes used by spies in the city, although no one has confirmed their purpose.
873069585:
Crying Leaf

Crying Leaf is an isolated elven village in Varisia's Mierani Forest. It is a day's travel from the ruins of the former elf city of Celwynvian, where the elves are engaged in a bitter struggle with the drow. As a result, it is used by the elves as a staging ground for their warriors, including contingents of Lantern Bearers, and is not entirely safe from drow raids.

The village consists mainly of simple, low stone and wooden structures, decorated with graceful carvings. It is protected by a wall of thorns. Most of the village's trade and supplies arrive from Kyonin through the use of magic—there is little contact with Riddleport and the rest of neighbouring Varisia. Non-elven visitors are rare.

The leader of the village is the sorcerer Eviana Nirgassan, who was appointed by Kyonin a decade ago following the assassination of her predecessor by the drow.
879592855:
Khoka

Khoka is a town in north-eastern Qadira on the southern banks of the Pashman River. It is the last stop within Qadira for merchants traveling eastward into the heart of the Empire of Kelesh, and the first way station for caravans entering the satrapy. Khoka is often full of caravans and mercenaries, many bargains take place in the town, and there are more rumors than could fill a hundred ears.

Khoka's permanent population is small, only 920 people, and residence permits are rarely granted. It is adjacent to a garrison for the Keleshite Imperial Forces, which enforces customs taxation under the command of Captain Jathbiyyah al-Istiyaz. It also contains a temple to Falayna.

Khoka is surrounded by a smattering of small villages in the foothills of the Zho Mountains, including the ratfolk village of Khurbresh located in a canyon less than a day's travel away. The former way station Pilgrim's Rest, a temple to Sarenrae abandoned due to an earthquake-induced shift in the nearby river, is located on the old road between them.

Caravans arriving from the Golden Path east of the town sometimes bring in travelers with a mysterious ailment that causes them to speak in tongues and their eyes to glow bright blue.

Slaves who are particularly devout worshipers of good-aligned deities fetch top coin in Khoka. The slaves are taken from town to the banks of the Pashman River and never seen again. They are destined for Nar-Voth, where they are part of the plans of the archdevil Moloch.
886091695:
Grandfather's Arms

2 locations:

  • Weeping Grandfather
888090745:
School of the North Song-Wind

2 locations:

895168385:
Daltus Academy

The Daltus Academy is a boarding school that occupies an entire block of the district where its campus, dormitories, and classrooms are located. Students at this academy are often involved in fights and are viewed unfavorably by the other residents of the district who tend to avoid them.
902383725:
Riekamesto

Riekamesto is a small town on Glacier Lake in the Irriseni province of Thronehold that has seen quite a bit of recent trouble.

As of 4713 AR, the Cold Sisters had recently discovered that Baroness Urszula and 13 of her councilors had been cheating Whitethrone by hiding tax revenues. Cold Sisters viciously killed these councilors by throwing them from the top floor of the Council House. Urszula was instead buried alive in a great brass coffin wrapped with bands of iron inscribed with strange sigils. They placed the coffin in the center of the town, and as of six months later Urszula's desperate cries and frantic pounding continued from within.

Sister Arminaela was temporarily made the town's administrator, and achieved hard work and discipline from the peasants and increased tax revenues.
912337945:
Fort Inevitable

Fort Inevitable is a small town just off the Crusader Road, sandwiched between the West Sellen River and the Echo Wood. It is ruled by members of the Hellknight Order of the Pike, but is otherwise unclaimed by any of the other political entities of the River Kingdoms.
927100985:
Reefclaw Run Market

The Reefclaw Run Market is a seafood market in the Old Korvosa district of Korvosa. The Reefclaw Run Market has existed for just under three centuries and is host to a range of seafood from Alikan oysters all the way up to jigsaw sharks. Fishing boat captains also often sell other things dredged up by their nets from the depths, including ancient artefacts and the remains of strange deep-sea creatures.

The Reefclaw Run Fountain provides water for the locals.
932545505:
Sun Dagger

Sun Dagger is a ruined solar observatory in the eastern Fierani Forest of Kyonin that was abandoned by the elves when they abandoned Golarion just before Earthfall.
937869745:
Cavern of Many Mouths

1 locations:
  • Troglodyte Caves
962537145:
Figwort Hollow

Figwort Hollow is a marsh that feeds into the Deepcut River in the Nesmian Plains of Nirmathas. The Foxclaws travel to the marsh every summer to harvest figwort, which can be used either as a poison or medicine. Due to a Nidalese ruin in the marsh, there are rifts to the Netherworld, forcing the Foxclaws to fight off numerous incursions.
963712865:
Shadow Spring

Shadow Spring is one of the northernmost hot springs in the Frostmire Fen, which can be found in the region of Sarkoris known as Frostmire. A small village of degenerate humans surrounds Shadow Spring (the village and the spring share the same name), populated by the descendants of evacuees from the nearby city of Dyinglight.
966282785:
Ehur

Ehur is a city in Qadira. Lying in the center of the Ketz Desert, this genie city is where denizens of the various Elemental Planes have their embassies and serves as a trading hub for these people within Qadira.
968683965:
Kintargo

992016415:
Lipror

Lipror sits on stilts over the Matra River delta where it meets the Embaral Ocean. Known as "The City of Lotuses", Lipror is one of many prosperous cities that make up the fertile region of Wide Water. Lipror is an active trade partner with the upstream city of Radripal, whose people share a common faith in Matravash and engage in annual pilgrimages to each others' holy sites.
995816195:
Temple of Asmodeus

3 locations:

1003723365:
Boursonne

Boursonne was a town in Lastwall that was besieged and destroyed by the undead hordes of Tar-Baphon following his escape from Gallowspire in 4719 AR, as well as by cultists of Urgathoa. It was the target of a rescue and evacuation operation led by the Pathfinder Society by request of Second Sword Knight Aylunna Varvatos.
1004065485:
Crownhold

A long-abandoned dwarven fortress is located in the Stormspear Mountains, about 5 miles north of the town of Glacier's Rest in the northwestern Hagreach of the Lands of the Linnorm Kings. As of 4724 AR, it was had been claimed by General Ordulf Bladecaller and named Crownhold.
1035036135:
Falling Runewheel

The Falling Runewheel is one of the cyclopean monuments of ancient Thassilon, built with the labor of enslaved rune and stone giants. It is located off the western coast of modern Varisia, which was the Thassilonian nation of Bakrakhan.
1048719905:
Ul-Angorn

Ul-Angorn is a small town on the Crown of the World, lying on the shores of Saltsea Lake. It is the largest settlement of Erutaki humans in the Ruun Uvas basin. Ul-Angorn is a resting spot for caravans along the Path of Aganhei between the High Ice and Ovorikheer Pass.

Ul-Angorn is prosperous for a town its size, and its inhabitants are inquisitive and garrulous.

Almas

1057526265:
Sand Dragon Inn

The Sand Dragon Inn sits along the southern side of the Golden Oasis. Travelers often stop here before exploring the city of Katapesh further on. A serpentine dragon perches above the main entrance of the inn.
1062687695:
Vira Grulios

Vira Grulios is a huge estate in the western part of Triam whose red-roofed towers rise above the Regiconan Walls. House Grulios lives within the estate.
1072349675:
Pegaduor

Located in the rural province of Kazuhn in Taldor, Pegaduor is a vast underground city that dates back to the Azlanti Empire and Earthfall.
1092548115:
Aaminiut

Aaminiut is the largest town in the decentralized Erutaki land of Hasanaliat. It is located on the southeastern end of Whitefang Bay at the mouth of the great Taraska River. Built on stilts, Aaminiut covers numerous small islets in the river delta and the nearby Hazalin Marshes.

Numerous Erutaki tribes are represented in Aaminiut, each tribe claiming its own islet or (for the smaller tribes) sharing an islet with a few other tribes. The largest clan in the city are the Miusunnit, whose claims in the city include control of the rare ice peppers found in the Hazalin Marshes. Many of the Erutaki migrate away from Aaminiut during the summer months, but most of the town's populace is permanent. A small colony of Ulfen traders from Almhult also lives in Aaminiut.
1106156895:
Rat-Taker's Palace

The Rat-Taker's Palace is the base of operations for the city's cleaners, responsible for maintaining the sewers, repairing pipes, and pest control. The building was abandoned after the earthquake, but they quickly returned to reclaim it, as its ostentatious appearance was too tempting to not move back in. Unfortunately, during their absence, a rat infestation took hold and continues to be a problem to this day.
1108161495:
Aspenthar

Aspenthar is Thuvia's second largest city-state and is the country's second port city on the Inner Sea after the capital, Merab. Generally seen as more dour in character than the capital, Aspenthar is under the rulership of the ambitious Prince Zinlo, who has decreed that martial training is mandatory for all citizens. Some worry that this training is in preparation for an invasion of one of the other city-states, or perhaps even an attempt to kidnap Artokus Kirran, the inventor of the sun orchid elixir.
1126051575:
Corvischior

Corvischior, sometimes spelt Corvischoir, is located in the south of the Ustalavic county of Varno. The town is now a shadow of its former self, the derelict county capital, and sits across the water from the equally forlorn Castle Corvischior, once the residence of the noble Tiriac family. Though still technically subject to the absentee Lord Ristomaur Tiriac, it has sat fallow for decades, though rumours speak of strange lights and unnerving shadows up at the castle. The most pessimistic residents whisper that Castle Corvischior, once known as Korsinoria Palace, is cursed, and whatever fell hex hangs over the castle has now settled over Corvischior as well.
1140148485:
Mount Kaltafarr

Mount Kaltafarr is the tallest peak in Taldor. This dormant volcano is located in the Southern Range of the World's Edge Mountains, east of the southern border town of Zimar. The ancient white dragon known as the Great White Wyrm is said to dwell on Mount Kaltafarr, even though he has not been spotted in over a century.
1150689415:
To Eat the World

To Eat the World is a famous restaurant in Westgate thanks to its chef, Sammael Rantore, who transforms the process of cooking and serving food into a dramatic spectacle that people even pay to watch. Only the wealthiest and most noble can afford to book a dinner here, which can take weeks or months, but the price is commensurate with the work and entertainment provided. Adventurers are hired to obtain the specific ingredients desired by their clients, and mementos of the most outlandish culinary feats adorn the walls. On one of the tables are statues of the Rantore family eating a cake shaped like Golarion.
1153231565:
Urglin

The predominantly orc city of Urglin (which means "Second Home" in the orc language) is the only population center of any sort in the Cinderlands region of Varisia's Storval Plateau.
1174713135:
Bailer's Retreat

Bailer's Retreat is a rough and tumble tavern near Citadel Volshyenek in Korvosa's Midland district. Bailer's Retreat serves as a drinking hole for many recently released criminals as well as many members of the Korvosan Guard. Brawls between the two groups are not uncommon and normally result in the former convicts returning to jail. Still, Bailer's Retreat serves as a hotbed of rumours concerning every sort of illicit activity in the city.
1175687565:
Suketh'ma

Close to Vargas Swamp, where the desert gives away to the swamp, underground water channels turn the ground to mud in a patch known as Suketh'ma. A large patch of sand conceals countless quicksand pits interspersed with muddy ditches, and travelers frequently disappear forever in the sucking pits. The mire is home to many sand eels.
1185271715:
River Runner Inn

The River Runner Inn is located in the Leeward District of Riddleport. It is a large structure, perched on a bluff overlooking the Velashu River. It is a somewhat pricey inn, but its true purpose is to serve as a clearing house for the smuggling and black market operations of crimelord Avery Slyeg.

1206765305:
Red Pyramid

A prominent landmark within the Twilight Gate area of the city of Katapesh, the Red Pyramid is an ancient structure that dates back to the "Golden City" age of Katapesh. In recent decades, it also served as the headquarters of the Scarlet Triad.
1214290925:
Turtleback Ferry

2 locations:

1232240475:
Spider nest

A 5-foot-wide and 25-foot-deep, densely webbed spider nest sits among the plant life of the Rostland Hinterlands, about a dozen miles southwest from Bokken's Hut. It is home to a spider as large as a pony, which hides the entrance of its lair with a trapdoor of hardened soil and waits for creatures to ambush. The nest's position is only given away by the skeletons of its prey, littering a 20-foot radius around the shaft hidden in their center. The scattered bones belong to deer, boars, bears, and humans.
1240197385:
Rivercroft

Rivercroft is a fishing town located at the confluence of the Usk and Gjurn Rivers in northern Nidal. Besides fishing, the town earns some of its income from river tolls, placing it in a better economic position than other towns in the region.

The town leader is Beldiore Chaintongue, who became interested in Rivercroft after a merchant discovered a shadow giant inside a massive shadowglass crystal and brought it to town. The shadow giant holds a book with runes similar to those found in Voyages into the Void. Beldiore has issued a call for adventurers to solve the mystery of this crystal and in the hopes of potentially earning a promotion for his efforts.
1252203805:
Tribulation

The small town of Tribulation can be found in the eastern edge of the Southern Range mountains in southeastern Taldor, on the far side of Emperor's Pass.
1255784835:
Darkreach

The settlement of Darkreach was established on the Vanji River around 30 years ago. It was intended to serve as a trading post between the mines of the Bandu Hills and the markets of Bloodcove in the Mwangi Expanse and of western Sargava.

At first it thrived—only to be razed to the ground by forces unknown. The people vanished, and all that was left of the buildings was the charred outline of the foundations. No natural fire cuts buildings off smoothly at ground level, and leaves no ash behind.

Some suspect the Aspis Consortium of wiping out a competitor; others wonder if the diamond miners had taken action against the tariffs charged on river traffic.
1260055875:
Atvan

Atvan is a small, peasant town on Glacier Lake in the Irrisen province of Bleakmarch. It is ruled by Baroness Baelara, whose husband, Gregouri Urvalane, is a notorious womanizer. The couple spend a great deal of time arguing, which is what got them banished to this remote settlement from Whitethrone.

The young women of Atvan who attract Gregouri's amorous attentions became targets of the baroness' extravagant rage. To avoid the baroness's wrath, Vaclav Konstantin proprietor of the Sealskinner Inn, has closed down his brothel and employs only local grandmothers in the inn's common room.

Atvan main business is to provide shelter, food, and entertainment for merchants sailing to and from Whitethrone. It is also well known that Atvan has coveted its neighbor Trezira's ice cleaving monopoly.
1260979305:
Macridi

The town of Macridi (pronounced ma-KRID-ee) is located in central Druma in the middle of the Palakar Forest, at the confluence of the Great Goldpan River and the Profit's Flow. It is primarily a logging town.

Although just as affluent as the other population centers of the Kalistrocracy, Macridi's residents do not feel the need to display their wealth as openly. This gives the wooded town a somewhat less opulent feel.
1270883155:
Ascanor Lodge

Ascanor Lodge is a private hunting compound for wealthy individuals located in the Shudderwood of the Ustalavic province of Lozeri.
1270934275:
Khari

Khari is the southernmost settlement of Cheliax, lying at the southern end of the Arch of Aroden and the Hespereth Strait that it once spanned. Since the independence of Sargava, Khari is the only Chelaxian city left on the continent of Garund. Formerly a Rahadoumi city, Khari is the largest settlement in the province of Kharijite in the Archduchy of Longmarch.

1302214355:
Blackhill's

Blackhill's is a tavern in Eastgate that at first glance looks like a thatched roof a foot off the ground, leading to a constantly ventilated cellar where Dege Blackhill creates his famous spicy drinks, popular with those from Numeria. Members of the Post Guard can also be seen there, spending their free time looking for missions to take on in the outskirts of town, and Dege is a good source of rumors, as he's quite the gossip. One of the rumors he'd like to see disappear concerns his past, specifically how he had to leave home after some kind of scandal, and the hints of nervousness he shows whenever someone brings up the subject suggest there's some truth to them.
1311744175:
Nagaiyamatsu

Nagaiyamatsu (pronounced nah-gie-yah-mah-tsoo) is a port city of the tengu kingdom of Kwanlai. It is prosperous as the nation's only port due to its location straddling the Sanga River.
1318323685:
Waldsby

Waldsby is a typical peasant village located near the Hoarwood Forest in the Irriseni province of Hoarwood.
1330324075:
Bent Rib Alley

Bent Rib Alley is a small street that ends in a cul-de-sac in a poor part of Magnimar's Keystone district.
1337305495:
Nesya

Nesya is the capital of the nation of Thieron located on the northern Kardaji Bay coast of the country.
1339970235:
Vhunsaub

Vhunsaub is thought to be an ancient underwater treasury of the Ghol-Gan, and is located among the Cannibal Isles of the Shackles. Many have tried to explore the ruin, but none have progressed past the entrance guarded by a magically compelled sea serpent.
1342526025:
Shrike Cascade

The Shrike Cascade is a waterfall on the Shrike River situated about a dozen miles downriver from the Crooked Falls. Here, the Shrike drops from a slippery 50-foot-high cliff, which offers an excellent view of the surrounding landscape, but makes any travel by boat impossible.
1346091375:
Holvirgang

The frost giant town of Holvirgang is located in the far north of the witch realm of Irrisen, hard against the icy tundra of the Crown of the World. Built into the side of a glacier, the town is composed of an outer walled bailey open to all, and the caverns of Holvir carved into the ice, where only the frost giants are allowed.
1348505075:
Wilting Rose

The Wilting Rose was a conservatory dedicated to the goddess Shelyn located on the outskirts of the Verduran Forest that has now fallen into serene ruin. Before its decline, the campus was known as Everrose School and was where young Taldans learned the art of sculpture and painting to a standard that matched the famed Rhapsodic College of Oppara. It has since become a mostly abandoned haven of werecreatures and hags.
1351777885:
Graybanks

5 locations:

1355270575:
Vaktai

Vaktai is one of the many mahajanapadas that dot the storied region of the Cradle in the Impossible Kingdoms of Vudra. Located on the coastline of the Embaral Ocean at the southern end of the Cradle, the city of Vaktai is controlled by rakshasas.

The rakshasas of Vaktai are also well-connected traders of luxury goods in the High Bluff district of Radripal.
1374413465:
Deep Sky

Deep Sky is a ruined fortress in the Napsune Mountains of Rahadoum that had once been settled by a group of Jistkan elementalists escaping the elemental storms at the Citadel of the Weary Sky with their elemental servants and golems. Under the elementalists' direction, it was constructed by four genies bound to obey their orders. However, the genies conspired to find a way to curse their masters, and succeeded in creating a curse that led to their masters' downfall.
1382842635:
Vaishau Ruin

The Vaishau Ruin is the grey, scorched remains of an old fortress located in the foothills of the Hungry Mountains of Lastwall, just a stone's throw from the Ustalavic county of Virlych. The ruin stands on the banks of the Mesmos River, which cuts the main pass through the mountains and connects Vigil with Gallowspire.
1382913985:
Serenity's Copse

Serenity's Copse is a community of dryads who live in a grove deep in Cheliax's Anferita Wood.
1384435985:
Temple of Nethys

The Temple of Nethys in Katapesh is an enormous pyramid with a multicolored flame that burns without any fuel. Inside, the vast space features walls decorated with the tenents of Nethys, while guards in masks and half-white, half-black robes watch to prevent thieves from entering. One of the rooms contains a library of arcane knowledge that the priests share among themselves to further their research into magical creations or their principles and their head priest Bale Politiz watches over these experiments.
1386202175:
Gallery of Innovation

The Gallery of Innovation is the Brigh temple in Absalom run by the impassive High Priestess Flindish Tanwhirl. Followers from all over the Inner Sea come to the temple, which is filled with engineers, blacksmiths, and metalworkers who push their skills to the limit working in the workshops inside. Constant classes and seminars are given by the clergy on the functions of inventions or new ideas that can be put into practice.

1408735235:
Alstone

Alstone is a fishing and farming village located on Flintyreach in the Ironbound Islands of the Lands of the Linnorm Kings. Alstone also has a nearby quarry.
1414230345:
Sisters of the Quill

The Sisters of the Quill is a congregation of women dedicated to preserving the written word, living in this two-story building. They are hired to translate books into other languages, and the business is run by Sister Masequa.
1418632595:
Besmara's Temple

The enormous Besmara's Temple is located on the mythical Holy Isle which stands at the center of the Cove of the Queen, the beautiful cove in the Besmara's Throne island in the Shackles. Half of the temple is submerged in the Holy Isle's tiny bay. Priests and acolytes in black silk work in the temple, performing ceremonies and welcoming those that end their Voyage there. An old woman, Laharra Seaspray, the current high priest of Besmara is always found within the temple.
1420490045:
Fort Fang

Fort Fang has been the headquarters of its region's kholo for decades. This fortress is built on top of Ancient Osirian ruins and was founded by the kholo slaver Hlasho. It houses around 500 soldiers and slavers and 1,500 slaves at any given time.

The fort is the target of frequent attacks by the Eagle Knights of Andoran as part of their abolitionist activities.

The status of this fortress is unknown since the widespread abolition of slavery in the Inner Sea region since 4719 AR.
1432472905:
Triela

Triela is a large town on Star Bay of the Inner Sea near the mouth of the Sellen River in south-eastern Andoran, close to its border with Taldor. It is the final stop for hundreds of lumberjacks every year who float their logs down from the Verduran Forest and flood the settlement with their hard-earned pay, spending it on company and drink. This has given Triela a reputation for debauchery, one which the wealthier citizens who reside on the bluff above the taverns and brothels have tried hard to overcome.

Last Rites

1460128845:
Yanmass

Yanmass is a Taldan city located along one of the trade routes that flow across the deserts of the Casmaron linking it with the heartland of the Empire of Kelesh, and from there with lands even further away. Yanmass's economy likewise relies on trade and vital services provided to caravans after their long treks across the continent, and the city hosts caravans from across the known world.
1468585025:
Magnimar

1472219685:
Jalunth

Jalunth is a village of Ekujae in the central Mwangi Jungle region of the Mwangi Expanse approximately 100 miles southeast of Nantambu. Due to years of difficult experience with outsiders, the residents rarely share even the name of their village with non-Ekujae. Finding Jalunth in the dense jungle terrain is difficult without appropriate local guides or skilled trackers.
1476558355:
Thrune's Chance

Thrune's Chance is a coastal hamlet on the edge of the North Plains in the west of Nidal on that nation's border with Cheliax. The hamlet is managed by the Chelaxian embassy in Pangolais. Most of its inhabitants are ambassadors or agents of House Thrune, under the command of Admiral Ferixia Rone. Each year, the embassy selects five Nidalese to work for a year at Thrune's Chance on joint activities between the two nations. If they complete the year successfully, the chosen ones can become Chelaxian citizens and live in the empire.

Thrune's Chance is an opportunity for the Umbral Court to rid itself of citizens dissatisfied with the government without provoking them to openly rebel. Those who take this opportunity are never allowed to return to Nidal.
1485708345:
River Inn

The River Inn is a prominent building in the Sprawl district of Katapesh. It was built in 3748 AR aiming to attract a higher cast of locals and visitors. It is a three-story building made of river stones and mortar with strong timber beams. The lanterns that flank its wide doors are lit with magical flames and the interior is suitable for nobles and rich merchants. Unfortunately rich merchants and nobles were never arrived in the district and the ownership of the River Inn has changed hands many times since its construction. The River Inn is still a magnificent building and has some of the best rooms in the city of Katapesh. Marvius Anquen, the current owner, works hard to keep the inn in good condition. Caravans, laborers, and adventurers are the most common customers. They enjoy having a nice place to stay without actually entering the city. A variety of fresh sea fish or river fish from River Scorpius, caught by Marvius's son Eldron, is always in River Inn's menu.
1498846265:
Lamashtu's Flower

Lamashtu's Flower is a hidden oasis and temple of Lamashtu located near the Osirian town of Eto. The site serves as the center of a kholo cult led by Nathrek the Pale and his consort Alashra.
1499390755:
Highhelm

Highhelm is among the most influential of the ancient and expansive dwarven settlements known as Sky Citadels. Constructed and founded in -4880 AR during the Age of Darkness within and beneath lofty Emperor's Peak in the Five Kings Range, it has remained the center of dwarven culture on Avistan for millennia.
1505459855:
Karakuru

The keyword exceeded the maximum length of 150 characters.

Karakuru is a small city located on the banks of Taldor's Porthmos River, just on the southern edge of the foothills of the southern World's Edge Mountains. Known as the City of a Hundred Fountains, Karakuru sits atop a high-pressure natural spring that brings water up to the surface. The citizens of Karakuru have harnessed this water source to power both the fountains that give the town its nickname as well as bathhouses that draw tourists of every class from throughout Taldor. Due to this constant influx of noble visitors arriving to soak in the ochre-colored mineral water in the hopes of alleviating aches and pains and treat chronic illnesses, the small tourist city is one of the wealthiest communities in Porthmos.

Karakuru is also home to Taldor's iconic fighting style known as rondelero, which represents a combination of peasant dances and noble weapons, a mixture that is a direct consequence of the interactions between people of all classes that happens at the bathhouses. The lightly armored fighting style uses an off-hand buckler to deflect blows and distract the opponent, while delivering precise strikes with a falcata. A small academy specializing in this combat art, known as the Rondelero School, is located in Karakuru and accepts up to a hundred students at any given time. Students typically come from a talent pool that includes locals, sons of the nation's wealthier families, and the most promising military officers in the empire. Though it is possible to learn rondelero at other combat schools that operate without the same exclusivity or punishing footwork drills, no institution has produced more skilled or respected masters of the fighting style.
1517143195:
Anjing

Anjing is a city located in the southeastern Heisong Forest of the Successor State of Lingshen. Anjing is a vast metropolis, its inhabitants numbering over sixty thousand.

According to legends, Anjing was the site of one of the challenges of the Tian Xia zodiac hero Tian Shen. Tales say the mythical hero lost an archery challenge due to his opponent's aggressive taunting, leading him to spend months mastering and balancing his qi through the practice of qigong. Tian Shen won the rematch through his renewed focus by striking the bullseye with every shot, each perfectly splitting his previous arrow. The constellation of the Archer is said to depict this event.
1521857485:
Thunderblood Peak

The volcano Thunderblood Peak, formally known as "Thisskarshai", is the tallest mountain in the Chenlun Mountains. It gets its name from the frequent earthquakes that occur around it.
1524884985:
Goldenflame

Goldenflame is an abandoned college town in southeastern Lastwall, immediately north of the Iomedaean Goldenflame College.
1530163735:
Field of Bones and Black Glass

The Field of Bones and Black Glass on Whyrlis Rock island in the Shackles is thought to be the field where the disastrous final battle between Raugsmauda and Gray Whyrlis occurred. The gruesome site is a magically dead zone about 4 miles in diameter. The place looks as though hundreds of skeletons clad in ancient armor were trapped and frozen in glassy black tar. A closer look reveals that the skeletal soldiers are fixed fast in solid obsidian as the earth beneath the victims boiled and solidified in seconds. Few adventurers dare to enter the place and snatch a sword or helm, and the field has remained undisturbed for thousands of years.
1534755705:
Deng

The ruins of the city of Deng can be found on the on the southern coast of Cheliax on the Inner Sea. The dead of the city are said to rise at night and reenact the lives they lived as if nothing had changed.
1536134305:
Sarini Estate

Sarini Estate, located in the Greens district of Kintargo, Ravounel, is the home of House Sarini. Most of its Thrune-loyalist residents fled Kintargo after the Kintargan Rebellion.

Elistia

The Hold of Elistia, or Fortress of the Bluffs, is a military outpost of the elven kingdom of Kyonin, located in the north-eastern part of the dwarven nation of the Five Kings Mountains. Elistia has a garrison of 500 elves commanded by Lord Alavar.

The Hold is protected by the Sentinels of Elistia, led by Lord Alavar in his role as Captain of the Sentinels. The Hold's elves are unfriendly to visitors, particularly orcs and dromaars, but assist mountaineers in need that they encounter in their patrols.

Cecaelia Swirl

The Cecaelia Swirl is a waterfall on the island of Besmara's Throne in the Shackles named after a tribe of cecaelia who inhabits the roiling surf. The octopoid creatures are lead by Ogolomae, a priest of Besmara, who is guarded by a group of skilled rangers who attack anyone who is not a devout worshiper of the goddess. A sea cave below the falls contains the former belongings of impious intruders.
1596806275:
Antler Lodge

Antler Lodge is the temple of Erastil, located in the urban city of Absalom, offering respite in its gardens to those who long for a rustic life. In the Eastgate district, a modest two-story structure can be seen with aesthetically pleasing wooden atlers at the entrance. The walls are magically reinforced to block out outside noise, granting tranquility to those inside. Instead of windows, illusions of rural landscapes adorn the walls, and the lodge is maintained by High Priestess Loys Zepah, an elderly woman who feeds sweets and confectionery like a grandmother to her grandchildren. Lately, strange things have begun to appear in the illusions, such as sick animals or burning landscapes, and adventurers are being sought to investigate the cause of this phenomenon.
1600362995:
Etherveil

The Etherveil is a section of several acres in the Arthfell Forest, one mile south of Elberwick Rise, where only silver bark trees grow and a constant fog fills the area. During the solstices, the grayish fog turns blue-green, indicating that something more supernatural is behind it, and map-eaters who have gone there to map it return with completely different maps, although all agree on the elven runes found in the area. Legend says that lovers who declare their love within the fog will live happily ever after, and more than one lover has been lost in the forest searching for it.
