1620693945:
Mount Keeba

Mount Keeba is the largest active volcano in the Shackles and is known in the archipelago as "the Smoker". It is located on the volcanic isle of the Smoker. Slow but steady rivers of lava course down the mountain's northern slope, and steam hisses violently as the molten material strikes the ocean waters. A steady plume of smoke rises from the huge crater at the volcano's summit. The volcano lends its name to a trio of seers called Keeba's Eyes, access to whom can be difficult and long in waiting.
1639473055:
Pagoda of the Mantis

The Pagoda of the Mantis is the temple to Achaekek in Ilizmagorti on Mediogalti Island. Situated in the Redshore district, it is the city's largest temple. It is open to all and unguarded during the daytime, but unbelievers are magically marked with the deity's religious symbol on their foreheads as soon as they step over the threshold and are promptly attacked by the rest of the congregation.
1641282915:
Hall to the Broken Dream

The Hall to the Broken Dream is the name of both a location hidden in Nidal and the cult devoted to the velstrac demagogue Vevelor that dwells within.

The hall itself seems to be constructed out of pure shadowstuff and the majority of its members are lampadarius velstracs who sheer off pieces of their own flesh to replace them with shadowstuff. Normally, lampadariuses serve the Midnight Lord Zon-Kuthon, but instead, those who dwell in the Hall to the Broken Dream worship Vevelor. Under the leadership of Skrialsiken, a velstrac who claims to be Vevelor's herald, the Hall to the Broken Dream has become a part of Nidalese society; every year, they send a delegation from the Hall to the capital city of Pangolais.

Normally any other religion would not be tolerated within Nidal but the adherents of the Hall to the Broken Dream insist that their existence is simply the defying of predestination that limits the other followers of Zon-Kuthon. Indeed, many amongst the Umbral Court dislike the Hall and its followers of Vevelor, they are however tolerated since most laypeople of Nidal cannot discern the subtle differences of philosophy between Zon-Kuthon and Vevelor and the Umbral Court wants to avoid the appearance of factionalism within its ranks. The Hall to the Broken Dream secretly recruits from amongst the people of Nidal searching for atheists and Kuthites who possess both dissatisfaction with their lot in life and the iron will to ascend to something more than human. Within the Hall to the Broken Dream these mortal followers of Vevelor undergo horrific fleshwarping procedures in an attempt to ascend into velstracs.
1644977585:
Uringen

An enigma among the settlements of the River Kingdoms, Uringen lies in the eastern regions of the country near the Embeth Forest. The settlement seems to exist in two realms, part of it "static" in reality, the other "unstuck"—disappearing and reappearing with the mists its inhabitants speaking Skald, a language not spoken in the River Kingdoms for ages.

Most outside the town theorize that the town is under some sort of foul magic or evil spell, but the true source of its strange behavior is a malfunctioning time-altering magical clocktower at the town's center that warped a third of the town to a pocket dimension between the Universe and the First World. The frequency of this phasing seems to be increasing, leading many in town to worry that it is moving closer to the First World.
1646895055:
Nordein Glacier

The Nordein Glacier is located in the central Fog Peaks of southern Galt. The mountain on which the glacier sits contains a hidden valley that is slowly being swallowed by the ice. Legends claim that an evil spirit called the Jarl of the North Wind lived near the glacier.

That legend turned out to be true because when adventurers arrived at the glacier they became trapped in a demiplane that they collapse using the Vernal Key. Since the Godsrain the grow of the glacier is beginning to threaten to consume the Fog Peaks in its entirety in a few years time.
1652757525:
Mimere

Mimere is a small town on the eastern bank of the Sellen River in the forest river kingdom of Hymbria. It serves as a trading outpost and boasts a sturdy dock, many small shops, a clean and comfortable inn, and a lively alehouse. River pirates seldom attack the peaceful village, because they have learned that at the smallest threat to the settlement, countless elven warriors descend from the trees, leaving only a few survivors to tell the story. The village has shrines to Calistria, Desna, Gozreh, and Nethys.

Hymbrian elves get along well with their visitors, as long as they stay in the trading outposts. Only elves are permitted beneath the trees of the Northern Hymbrian Forest; others are slain on sight.
1700744035:
Nivakta's Crossing

Nivakta's Crossing is a village at the very southern edge of Rostland situated on the northern bank of the Shrike River and overlooking the Stolen Lands of the River Kingdoms to its south.
1711612335:
Surtse

Surtse is the westernmost town in Almhult. It is located on the southern coast of Cape Almhult on the shores of the Steaming Sea, to the west of Asre. It lies in the shadow of the Hellrung Mountains further west.
1717818345:
Iadenveigh

Iadenveigh is a reclusive and isolated town of woodsmen and hunters in southern Numeria on the upper reaches of the Dagger River. Located on the northern outskirts of the Echo Wood, the citizens of Iadenveigh reject any association with the Technic League that controls the Numerian capital of Starfall.

1741871085:
Carrion Hill

The city of Carrion Hill in eastern Ustalav is a strange place, resting atop an unnatural and unsettling mound. Why people choose to live here is unclear, but for some reason they always have. Varisians first settled Carrion Hill in 2397 AR; prior to this, Carrion Hill was a holy site for the local Kellid tribes.
1750783935:
Buwayang

Buwayang is a settlement in western Minata led by Ikluss the Ghostmarked, an intimidating white-scaled lizardfolk who declared herself Warrior-King of Buwayang and lines her hall with her fallen foes' skulls.
1750881355:
Cahshil

Cahshil is a hamlet built upon a particularly deep wetland in southern Sargava by a community of Song'o halflings originally from the Laughing Jungle who fled the escalating warfare between the lizardfolk and boggards. These halflings are subsistence hunters and rarely trust or interact with outsiders, giving rise to the erroneous rumours that they command terrible spirits and eat human flesh. The tojanida Guughwa is an occasional ally and frequent bully of Cahshil's residents, and often demands hospitality and attention from them.

The state of Cahshil is unknown since the Vidric Revolution removed Sargava's government.

1763031465:
Saraby

Saraby is a small town on the Thundering River in the Irriseni province of Wintercrux. It is a bustling peasant town which accepts a number of Varisian merchants that have the courage to travel the few treacherous passes across the Kodar Mountains into Irrisen. The brave merchants mainly deliver grain and foodstuff and are richly rewarded. Saraby is ruled by Baroness Zoja who carefully accounts the volumes of trading goods moving through her town. Much of the precious cargo moves on to Dalun to the west and eventually is delivered to the militant settlements and camps of the Verge. The rest, however, is sent east along a route known as the Saraby Trail.

The Saraby Trail to the east follows no visible roads. It follows the Thundering River until reaching the Kodar Mountains and then heads north towards Hope Lost. From Hope Lost some experienced groups head directly east to Sosulka fighting the beasts of the tundra and the fearful white-furred perytons that prowl the skies but most of the groups take one of the well traveled roads that meander through the Skala Foothills to Zlatomesto. There they join a heavily guarded caravan of ore and travel to Veshtak. Bendiks Poljarny and Velna Elishky are veterans of the Saraby Trail.

1796530985:
Rune Mausoleum

The Rune Mausoleum is one of the cyclopean monuments of ancient Thassilon, built with the labor of enslaved rune and stone giants. It was located in the southwestern part of the Thassilonian nation of Cyrusian, now western Varisia near the Mierani Forest.
1804389485:
Tower of Twin Truths

1 locations:

1804455285:
Dawnflower Library

The Dawnflower Library is a temple of Sarenrae and library located on 10 Dawnflower Street in the town of Otari, on the southern coast of the Isle of Kortos. Its high priestess is Vandy Banderdash, a talkative halfling woman. While the temple also hosts shrines to Cayden Cailean, Erastil, and Gozreh, most of the building is dedicated to its publicly available collection of books. The temple also offers medicine, magical healing, and divine spellcasting services.
1809489125:
Kintargo Keep

1816865835:
Infinite Ossuary

The Infinite Ossuary is a ruined fortress in the Nesmian Plains of what is now Nirmathas controlled by undead, constructs, and elementals. The original fortress was built on top of an onyx mine, with the strategic purpose to secure the spell components to animate the armies of Tar-Baphon.

As of 4717 AR, the Infinite Ossuary still held hundreds of preserved corpses ready to be raised as undead reinforcements, as well as dormant undead and the sleeping General Kros Catternine, a graveknight.
1826629475:
Mount Langley

Mount Langley is the fourth-tallest peak of the Five Kings Range at 17,400 feet and is the site of one of the Carved Kings sculptures, bearing the visage of King Taggrick I. The mountain is located at Kingtower Pass and is one of four mountains surrounding the city of Taggoret, alongside Mount Onik to its east, Mount Carissa to its south, and Mount Soryu to its west.
1836877905:
Goblin King's Court

The Goblin King's Court is a series of wooden walkways and towers resembling a castle amidst the flooded houses of The Puddles. It is where Zusgut resides, acting as king. The goblins' short stature makes them reluctant to walk through the wet streets, so they hop from house to house to reach the court. Zusgut has advisors from all over Golarion who come to the city following tales of his fame. The goblins have devised a system that repairs and removes planks depending on the tide, and they have prepared a large stage where they perform daily. Therefore, it is easy to find the court simply by listening for the tremendous noise they make shouting during theatrical performances.
1842404945:
Old Temple of Erastil

1 locations:
  • Verennie's Lair
1842803965:
Mormouth

Mormouth is a small village on the Sellen River in the River Kingdom of Pitax.
1847277675:
Aelyosos

Aelyosos is the westernmost city of note in the archipelago of Iblydos in the Obari Ocean. It serves as the gateway to Iblydan culture and hospitality to most travelers on the Obari Crossing, and due to its many gardens and the tireless work of its citizens, is an overall peaceful place to rest and conduct trade.
1848859305:
Hanging City of Teskra

The Hanging City of Teskra is an ancient ruin of the Jistka Imperium, located in the modern-day nation of Rahadoum in the Napsune Mountains.
1858617695:
Almas University

6 locations:

1862648405:
Kokutang

Kokutang was the capital of Yamasa, one of the two lost kingdoms that now make up the Sodden Lands. The city was destroyed by the Eye of Abendego. Today there is a town on the surface, with a complex of caves and passages beneath, most of which are flooded.

The site is a seasonal gathering place for the Koboto, the cannibalistic descendants of the people of Yamasa; visitors risk being eaten.
1864403355:
Devil's Own

The Devil's Own is a shipyard in the Docks district of the city of Absalom. The shipyard specializes in war galleys used by Absalom's navy and sold to the markets throughout the Inner Sea. Its owner, Lord Archych of House Dureanz is a loyal Chelaxian. He employs the worst thugs and pushes around his considerable weight at every opportunity. The machinations of Lord Archych and those of his rival Goodmiss Alidane of House Candren, manager of the Sea King Shipyard, are the main source of intrigue and violence in the Docks.
1883153205:
Sanctuary of Shelyn

The Sanctuary of Shelyn is the smallest independent temple in the city of Korvosa. Dedicated to Shelyn, the goddess of beauty and art, it also ranks as amongst Korvosa's most beautiful buildings. The sanctuary sits in the heart of the North Point district; it is a square building set around a central courtyard filled with beautiful statuary and murals, as well as an array of enticing plants. While the building itself is also replete with fine artwork, it never feels cluttered.
1887413365:
Tuvar

Tuvar is the de facto capital of the Ulaagor Clans of the Erutaki, located on the shore of Ulaagor Bay at the outlet of the Niiset River. This town is the site of the Ulaagor clan moots and trade fairs.
1891387885:
Rikhist

Rikhist is a rough border town in the far east of Qadira's Maharev region. Located near the headwaters of the Meraz River and just west of the Zho Mountains that mark Qadira's border, Rikhist is the largest centre of Rovagug worship in all Qadira despite the best efforts of Satrap Xerbystes II and his predecessors to completely eliminate it. Like a cockroach, Rikhist has withstood all attempts to stamp it out and it is supported by both orc and cave giant tribes that dwell in the surrounding mountains. The town is home to all manner of outcasts, thieves, blasphemers, and murderers of many races, including not just humans and orcs but also numerous bugbears and kholo. Rikhist is ruled by an axe-wielding sadist named Magyarsh, a riftmarked cambion warpriest devoted to Rovagug.
1891975205:
Arbo

Arbo is a mostly abandoned limestone quarry village in the western Swardlands of the Isle of Kortos, located north of the Irr River on the western edge of the Inland Hills on a farm track that connects the northern Husna Crossroads to Stump south of Arbo.
1920344745:
Tradecross

Tradecross is a small collection of farmhouses and an inn in the Plains territory of Molthune. The town is mainly known for the occasional appearance of the mysterious Tradecross Peddler.
1924268865:
Ravengro

The Ustalavic settlement of Ravengro is located in the west of the county of Canterwall, close to the contentious border with the Hold of Belkzen.
1924485435:
Sardis Township

Sardis Township is a large town on the upper reaches of the River Porthmos in eastern Taldor's Porthmos Gap.

The prefectural capital of the Porthmos Prefecture, Sardis extends for well over a mile in every direction as it was formed as a dozen small mining communities that grew together around rich veins of lead and other minerals. The settlement sits on the chaotic, polluted landscape that stretches just a few miles inland from the South River. Technically, the piers, refineries, and warehouses are also part of the township's domain. Despite being depressing and miserable, Sardis generates a great deal of wealth, trading high-quality metals for foodstuffs to supplement their own meagre crops.

Although not known as a trade hub, Sardis does profit from trade caravans and travelling merchants. Centaur tribes from the Whistling Plains sometimes make the trek this far into Taldor for trade and some even choose to remain. Many are enlisted as enforcers by the local Grand Duke.

Sardis Township is home to both the infamous Porthmos Prison and the governor of the Porthmos Prefecture, Grand Duke Thestro Briarsmith. The former is populated with the enemies of the latter.
1932914465:
Kerse

1943476485:
Spry's Heart

Spry's Heart is a dark and tangled part of the Embeth Forest in the River Kingdom of Uringen. The area seems to attract evil fey and corrupt good ones, and is the home of the grig Mather Nithra.
1952566885:
White Pass

The White Pass is the widest pass through the northern Zho Mountains, and is located in Qadira on its border with Taldor. It forms the western extent of the Golden Path before its terminus at Katheer.

The Battle of Urfa, the first armed conflict between Taldor and Qadira, took place in -4 AR near the White Pass.
1962249025:
Shaping Bluffs

The Shaping Bluffs are a two-mile-long geological feature along the Sellen River and the border it forms with Galt, and is known for the mysterious tendency of its bluffs to constantly reshape their faces. The steep cliffs expose red stone that changes erratically regardless of any winds or waters and can hide or reveal caves at water level.

The cliffs house a camp of dwarven stone shamans and geomancers, led by Hromcul Stetruk, who have stabilized their location enough to continue studying the phenomenon. The Shaping Bluffs are also used by river pirates who take advantage of the transient nature of caves that appear and disappear along the water level to raid traffic along the Sellen River.
1962855535:
Tower of Slant Shadows

Located in the southwestern plains of Nidal, the Tower of Slant Shadows is a rugged iron edifice that juts diagonally from the umber grassland. It is believed that it sinks several times its own height into the earth beneath it. A mystery to most, the tower is in fact one of the almost-forgotten Star Towers, constructed untold millennia ago by Zon-Kuthon (then Dou-Bral) as part of the process necessary to confine Rovagug within Golarion.
1970221365:
Matriculum's Charge

Matriculum's Charge, formally known as Hillsprung, gets its name from the dragon that guards it, Matriculum.
1975993145:
Haseong

Haseong is the cosmopolitan capital of Hwanggot in eastern Tian Xia. This bustling port city is known for its diverse crafts, artworks, and industries, and the many festivals that promote and celebrate them. Each of its many festivals feature competitions and are open to outsiders.
1980222865:
Bearleg Lakes

The Bearleg Lakes are an area of moorland dotted with lakes and rivers southwest of the Gaarjuk Hills, in the Crown of the World.
1984692045:
Cave of a Thousand Stars

The Cave of a Thousand Stars is a system of caves created by a moot of muldnal agathions in an area of Vudra where the planar boundaries between the Universe and Nirvana grow thin. It is a place where the otherworldly perfection of Nirvana seeps into Golarion as a palpable presence and, as such, is sought out by monks and scholars alike.
1991097995:
Riverton

Riverton is a large village located on the banks of Kallas Lake in the southern River Kingdoms.
1992537075:
Ganda-Uj

Ganda-Uj is the treetop lair of the monkey goblins of Mediogalti Island. It consists of precariously balanced bark huts, linked by swaying vines and bridges. Scholars believe that the current site is the fifth lair to bear the name; the other four were accidentally burned down by the fire-loving goblins.

The goblins delight in attacking travellers, using nets and poison darts to take prisoners, who are then presumably sacrificed to whatever demons the goblins worship. It is best to travel through monkey goblin territory in large, well-armed groups.
2013747405:
Humbright House

Humbright House was one of a number of smaller shrines to Aroden that once dotted Kintargo's Temple Hill district but has been abandoned since the god's death.
2014618435:
Thornhearth

Thornhearth is a small thorp in Geb just south of the Axanir River.
2019688285:
Hothouse

The Hothouse is the administrative headquarters of the Petal District, where the Conclave of Flowers meets. It also serves as a greenhouse, housing plants imported from around the world, symbolizing the transparency of the council's actions. In practice, however, the interior is uncomfortable and unpleasant, also serving as a metaphor for the interests behind the councilors' actions, which are directed by the local aristocracy.
2041751685:
Thunderhold

The fortress of Thunderhold on the isle of Flintyreach in the Lands of the Linnorm Kings, is ruled by the charismatic hill giant chieftain Gorgnak who leads a semi-loyal band of hill giant, ettin, and troll warriors. The giants who live in the mountains of the island's interior are the greatest threat to the dromaar community of Averaka. The Averaka giant slayers have dedicated themselves to the eradication of their monstrous neighbors.
2046748825:
Icebow Bridge

The village of Icebrow Bridge is a darkly scenic and quaint little village that sits in the centre of the shadowy nation of Nidal. Icebrow Bridge would be an unremarkable little village were it not for the Library Without Light, a vast collection of ancient, pre-Earthfall Azlanti and Thassilonian texts that were brought by fleeing survivors of this ancient apocalypse to Nidal. The ancient scrolls and books cover a wide variety of often obscure topics but have zero cohesion as a collection, as they were whatever texts their long-dead owners valued enough to take with them when fleeing their homes. The head librarian Hale Craggox controls access to the Library Without Light. Due to the prestige of the library, Icebrow Bridge is considered quite a desirable posting for agents of the Umbral Court.
2057656605:
Burn

Located in the western part of the Keystone district, the area now known as the Burn was once home to the Varisian Council. When esteemed painter Goren Andosalu went mad and set his studio on fire, the fire spread to several other fine buildings in the area, killing over 48 people. Andosalu was never confirmed as one of the dead.

This destruction so close to their home building caused the Varisian Council to change where it convenes. Between this and the ghosts of these 48 residents that are rumored to haunt the location, most locals avoid the area. A local group of cat burglars named the Gargoyles uses the old Varisian Council building as a headquarters.
2061906165:
Dekarium

The Chelaxian settlement of Dekarium rests on the northeastern bank of Lake Sorrow near the mouth of the Tomarsulk River. Across Lake Sorrow lies Cheliax's capital, Egorian, while the Barrowood begins just west of the city. The city is renowned for its highly skilled fishermen and abundant fey-blooded sorcerers. Local legend tells that the townsfolk of Dekarium are descended from nereids. A druid patrols the land between the town and the forest, mounted on a griffon named Ironwing Kazi, seeking out evildoers and those wishing to make sacrifices to their diabolic masters in the Barrowood. Much of the village secretly worships Gozreh, including its mayor Lalita Jireen, who manages the town's image and tithes to House Thrune.
2068867875:
Riddleport Light

The Riddleport Light is built on a lonely crag at the edge of Riddleport's eastern ridge, south of the Rotgut District. The lighthouse provides guidance to the safety of Riddleport Harbor and away from the treacherous rocks just to the east of the harbor's mouth. It is reached by a narrow winding stairway and is the home of a sorcerer named Gebediah Krix.
2069270785:
Citadel of Flame

The Citadel of Flame was the headquarters of a cult of the archdevil Moloch that arose in the Meraz Desert of Qadira three centuries ago. It is located in the western Meraz Desert, near the shores of Obari Ocean.

Thought to have been annihilated and covered by a stand storm, the citadel was rediscovered by the Pathfinder Society in 4709 AR. Upon sending Pathfinders to investigate, it was discovered that the cult was resurgent and under the control of a powerful cleric of Moloch named Hafshi Al-Moloch, who hoped to open a permanent planar portal to Malebolge. The Pathfinders defeated the cult and returned its magical idol of Moloch to the Grand Lodge in Absalom.
2073420245:
Penitentiary

The Penitentiary was conceived as a prison in Corentyn where non-violent prisoners would be sent to silently reflect on their crimes, find inner peace, and be reformed upon release. Currently, it houses the city's forbidden religious relics and serves as a detention center for those who cause disturbances in West Drenches.
2074969445:
Mirazha

Years ago, the town of Mirazha in Katapesh was a waypoint for weary travelers, then an mysterious disaster struck the town. Mirazha was deserted and travelers found the bodies of the townsfolk in the oasis on the edge of town. The bodies were desiccated and withered, all the moisture seemingly drained. For a time, Mirazha stood abandoned. Eventually the desert sands covered over the town, the oasis dried up, and the town was forgotten. Recently, an evil has revived the memory of Mirazha and its doomed inhabitants. Travelers report seeing a little town on the edge of a sparkling oasis, but if someone enters the cursed town, the town and the new victim vanish from sight. Adventurers in the area have yet to spot the cursed town, but sometimes they find the mummified bodies of the victims, buried in the sand.

Gardens of Delight

The Gardens of Delight, or Garden of Delights, are a gigantic tiered group of gardens located in the Holoma province of Juttar. The gardens are presided over by Juttar's Grand Omwa Holo Enyana's palace, and host not only beautiful plants but also a large menagerie of unusual and magical animals. The wildlife here is entirely tame, and celestial hybrids of dinosaurs native to Holomog display particularly unusual and eye-catching characteristics.

2114297005:
Chotilema's Boudoir

Chotilema's Boudoir is an underground and underwater cavern in the western foothills of the mountains of Zi Ha in Wanshou. Once the lair of the bog witch Chotilema, it is filled with all manner of undead abominations, molds, and slimes that have never seen the light of day.
2118972425:
Krega

Krega is a small town on the western edge of the Estrovian Forest in the nation of Mendev that acts as a stop on the crusade route between the port city of Egede to the east and the front of the Worldwound to the west. Because the Fourth Mendevian Crusade attracts warriors from all over the Inner Sea region, Krega has a diverse population, most of whom work to supply the crusaders with anything they might need, from weapons and armor to food, shelter, and more earthly pleasures.

2126848465:
Lenyenko Dark

The cavernous failed mine in the Icerime Peaks of Brevoy known as the Lenyenko Dark leads to a pre-Earthfall complex that dates to Koloran, the ancient cyclopean empire of Casmaron. After a series of unfortunate events, the complex was sealed by the Brevic government around 4639 AR.
2131675675:
Sundown Street

Sundown Street is a curving street near the city wall in the Ivy District, a few blocks from the bustling Flower Street Market. This street is renowned for housing the three most prestigious and sought-after tailors in all of Absalom: Ambroz Black, Al Amir Kai, and Madame Theodora Signe. It takes more than just money to gain the attention of these busy tailors; one also has to invite them to dinners, performances, and galas to secure a commission. The other cosmetics shops on the street benefit indirectly from the "big three"'s reputation, and only the elite of the elite frequent this area regularly.
2132563235:
Vaneo Dioso

Vaneo Dioso is a mansion that once belonged to House Krafanis, but when that line was eradicated, the magnificent structure fell into the hands of House Dioso and remains so to this day.
2135056425:
Archerus Manor

Ehur

2136352235:
Macini

The Chelaxian port city of Macini, located on northern shores of the Inner Sea in the Archduchy of Longmarch, is located about halfway between the settlements of Hinji and the Corentyn. It is best known for its prestigious Law Court, to the point that people of Cheliax's southern coast refer to any fair, well-reasoned person as being "sharp as a Macinian magistrate".
2139943525:
Seven-Fangs Bastion

The Seven-Fangs Bastion is a small, millennia-old ruined castle dating back to the Age of Darkness.
2140761845:
Heger

Heger is a Qadiran city in the Meraz Desert region that separates Qadira from the rest of the Empire of Kelesh. The Meraz Desert once used to be fertile land before it was sapped of life by the Heralds of Dust. Heger is located along the coastline where the desert meets the Obari Ocean, between the cities of Izzet to the north and Husanah to the south.
